summary: "Gingival ablation" is not a recognized etiological agent for gingival recession; instead, mechanical abrasion, inflammatory processes, and transgingival furcations are well-established causes in dental pathology. This conclusion relies on standard dental knowledge, even though the original search results were irrelevant to this topic.
